cases.prompt: Grill me on this implementation plan until the risky branch decisions and verification gaps are fully resolved.
Refactor this handler to remove duplication and make the error semantics clearer.
I want to fix this checkout bug with TDD. Start with the most important behavior slice.
This regression changes the API response body. The endpoint is covered by a fast integration test, and I want the safest fix.
This test fails only in CI and I do not understand why yet.
Should this plugin system stay simple, or should we introduce a strategy pattern?
Critically review this codebase and create a phased plan to simplify the architecture, make it more agent friendly, and improve testing at the right seams.
Critically review this repo's tests, remove bad or redundant coverage, suggest better seam-level tests, and create a phased plan aligned with behavior-first TDD.
Clean up this codebase: deduplicate code, remove unused code, fix weak types, untangle cycles, remove legacy fallbacks and AI slop, then implement high-confidence recommendations.
We ingest append-only events and query them by time range; should we keep plain Postgres or move toward Timescale?
Wrap this third-party billing SDK in Effect without leaking the raw client everywhere.
This Rust API keeps borrowing data too long. I need a cleaner ownership boundary.
Write a clear PR description that explains the problem, the user-facing changes, the implementation, and how to verify it.
I got code review feedback and I am not sure whether it is technically correct for this codebase.
Before I say this fix is done, what do I need to run to prove it?
This refactor is risky and I want a separate branch workspace before touching the current tree.
Add a new admin export workflow across the API, queue worker, and dashboard UI. Start by inspecting the repo and give me a short plan before editing.
Implement a larger feature with one backend slice, one docs slice, and one test-fixture slice; preserve context and split independent work where it makes sense.
This is a new internal workflow tool with unclear UX and integration shape. Inspect constraints, then prototype the thinnest useful path before locking the implementation plan.
This will take multiple sessions and several agents. Inspect first, then create a durable exec plan in the repo before implementing.
The branch is done and I need to decide whether to merge it, open a PR, or keep it around.
Address this review feedback, implement the valid changes, prove the branch is ready, describe the PR, and finish the branch workflow.
Create a new skill and keep the entrypoint small while moving heavy material into references.
